title: acer amplum subsp. bodinieri
intro: Acer amplum subsp. bodinieri is a plant species belonging to the family Sapindaceae, commonly referred to as the rhus tree. The plant is native to China and is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Acer amplum subsp. bodinieri include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve drying and processing the leaves, stems, and roots, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
